stories.title: Global quick-add window with customizable shortcut
stories.description:
A frameless floating window triggered by a global macOS keyboard shortcut (default: Cmd+Shift+Space) that allows adding tasks from anywhere in macOS without switching to the app.

Window: Frameless BrowserWindow, ~500px wide, auto-height, rounded corners, themed to match the active theme (surface bg, border, foreground text). Contains: auto-focused title input ('Add a task...'), destination dropdown defaulting to 'My Day' listing all projects. Enter submits and closes. Escape closes without submitting.

Task creation: Uses the same preload bridge and IPC handlers as the main window. Creates task with default status (Not Started), respects 'new_task_position' setting for order_index, sets is_in_my_day: 1 when My Day is selected. After creation, sends 'tasks-changed' IPC event to main window to trigger silent store refresh without showing/focusing the main window.

Global shortcut: Registered via Electron's globalShortcut API in main process. Default: Cmd+Shift+Space. Customizable in Settings > General with a 'Record shortcut' button. Conflict detection: known macOS shortcuts (Cmd+Space/Spotlight, Cmd+Shift+3,4,5/Screenshot, Cmd+Tab/App Switcher, Cmd+Option+Esc/Force Quit, etc.) are blocked with message 'This shortcut is reserved by macOS (Feature Name) and can't be used.' Unknown conflicts (registration failure from other apps) warn and ask to overwrite.

App lifecycle: On macOS, closing the main window hides it instead of quitting the app. Process stays alive, global shortcut stays registered. Dock icon click re-opens main window via app.on('activate'). Cmd+Q truly quits and unregisters shortcuts. No tray icon.

Auth guard: Shortcut is a no-op if no active user session exists.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Cmd+Shift+Space opens a small frameless floating window from any app on macOS
Window contains auto-focused title input and destination dropdown (My Day + all projects)
My Day is selected by default in the destination dropdown
Enter creates the task and closes the window
Escape closes the window without creating a task
Task is created with default status, correct project, and respects new task position setting
When My Day is selected, task has is_in_my_day: 1
Main window task list refreshes silently after quick-add without being shown or focused
Shortcut is customizable in Settings > General via a Record button
Known macOS shortcuts are blocked with a message naming the specific macOS feature
Unknown shortcut conflicts warn and ask to overwrite
Shortcut persists across app restarts (saved in settings)
Closing the main window hides it — app stays alive, shortcut stays registered
Clicking dock icon re-opens the main window
Cmd+Q fully quits the app and unregisters the shortcut
Shortcut does nothing if user is not logged in
Quick-add window matches the current app theme

stories.title: Smart input parsing with @label, p:priority, and d:date syntax
stories.description:
Add inline smart parsing to all task creation inputs: the global quick-add window (story #20), the Backlog add-task input (TaskListView), and the My Day add-task input (MyDayView). The existing AddTaskInput component should be extended to support this — all three locations use it or should use the same parsing logic.

Shared popup component: Create a new shared component InputSuggestionPopup that renders a small floating list below the input. Max 5 items visible. Each item has a color dot on the left and a label on the right. Arrow Up/Down navigates, highlighted item has bg-foreground/6. Enter or Tab selects the highlighted item. Escape dismisses the popup and leaves the typed text as-is (literal title text — this is the escape hatch so users can type things like 'stop:new things' without triggering operators). After Escape, that specific operator instance (tracked by character position) does not re-trigger; a new operator at a different position triggers normally. The popup uses a portal to document.body with z-[9999], positioned directly below the input using getBoundingClientRect(). Matches the app theme (surface bg, border, foreground text). Animation: motion-safe:animate-in fade-in zoom-in duration-100.

@label syntax: When the user types @ anywhere in the input, the popup opens showing up to 5 labels from the current project, filtered by substring match (case-insensitive) on what follows the @. For example, typing @wo shows labels containing 'wo' (e.g., 'Work', 'Workout'). Each item shows the label's color dot + name. At the bottom of results, always show a '+ Create typed text' option with a preview color dot (the auto-assigned color). This create option only appears when the typed text after @ is non-empty. When the user selects a label (existing or create): (1) remove the @text from the input value, (2) add a LabelChip component to a chip area rendered below the input, (3) close the popup. Multiple labels can be attached — each @ triggers a new popup. The chip area is a flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 row below the input. Each chip has an X button to remove it (or Backspace when the input is empty removes the last chip).

Auto-create labels: When the user selects '+ Create name', create a new label in the current project via window.api.labels.create(). Auto-assign a color from a curated palette of 12 visually distinct colors: ['#ef4444', '#f59e0b', '#22c55e', '#3b82f6', '#8b5cf6', '#ec4899', '#14b8a6', '#f97316', '#06b6d4', '#84cc16', '#6366f1', '#e11d48']. Pick the first color not already used by any label in the current project. If all 12 are used, cycle from the start. The label's order_index should be max existing + 1. After creation, the label is immediately assigned to the chip area as if it were an existing label.

p:priority syntax: When the user types p: (must be preceded by a space or be at the start of input), the popup opens showing priorities filtered by prefix match on what follows. Priority names and their values: none(0), low(1), normal(2), high(3), urgent(4). Also accept aliases: medium(2). Prefix matching: p:u shows urgent, p:h shows high, p:n shows none and normal, p: with nothing after shows all 5. Each item shows the priority color dot + name. When selected: (1) remove p:text from input, (2) show a PriorityBadge in the chip area below the input, (3) close popup. Only one priority at a time — selecting a second one replaces the existing badge. Priority badge in chip area has an X to remove it (resets to none/0).

d:date syntax: When the user types d: (same space/start-of-input rule as p:), the popup opens showing date presets filtered by prefix match: today (today's date), tomorrow (tomorrow's date), monday through sunday (next occurrence of that weekday, including today if it matches). Also accepts explicit dd/mm/yyyy format — when the typed text matches the pattern of digits and slashes (e.g., d:22/03/2026), validate it as a real date and show it as a selectable option in the popup with a Calendar icon. Each preset item shows a Calendar icon (lucide) + label + the actual date in dd/mm/yyyy format as secondary text. When selected: (1) remove d:text from input, (2) show a date chip in the chip area (Calendar icon + formatted date), (3) close popup. Only one date at a time — selecting a second replaces it. Date chip has X to remove. The date is stored as ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) in the task's due_date field.

Operator detection: Parse the input text to find operators. An operator is triggered when the user types @, p:, or d: that is either at the start of the input or preceded by a space. Track the cursor position to determine which operator is active. Only one popup can be open at a time. If the user moves the cursor away from the operator text, close the popup.

On submit (Enter key): If the popup is open and an item is highlighted, Enter selects that item (does NOT submit the task). If no popup is open, Enter submits the task: (1) use the remaining input text (after all operator texts have been removed by selections) as the task title, (2) create the task with the selected priority (default 0 if none) and due_date (null if none), (3) after task creation, call addLabel(taskId, labelId) for each attached label, (4) clear the input and chip area.

Command palette update: In useCommandPaletteSearch.ts, replace the due: operator with d:. Remove due: as a recognized prefix. The d: operator should support the same values as before: today, week, overdue.

State management: The parsing state (active operator, attached labels, selected priority, selected date) should live in a custom hook useSmartInput that wraps the input logic. This hook is used by AddTaskInput and the quick-add window's input. It manages: attachedLabels: Label[], selectedPriority: number | null, selectedDate: string | null, popupState: { type: '@' | 'p:' | 'd:' | null, query: string, position: {top, left}, suppressedPositions: Set<number> }.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Typing @ in any add-task input triggers a popup with up to 5 matching labels
Popup filters labels by substring match as user types after @
Arrow keys navigate popup, Enter/Tab selects, Escape dismisses and keeps text literal
After Escape, the same operator instance does not re-trigger; a new one at a different position does
Selecting a label removes @text from input and shows a LabelChip below the input
Multiple labels can be attached to one task
+ Create name option appears at bottom of popup when typed text after @ is non-empty
Creating a new label auto-assigns a color from a curated 12-color palette, skipping colors already in use
New label is scoped to the project selected in the destination dropdown
Typing p: triggers a popup with up to 5 matching priorities
Priority popup filters by prefix match (p:u -> urgent, p:h -> high)
Selecting a priority removes p:text from input and shows a PriorityBadge below the input
Only one priority at a time — second selection replaces the first
Typing d: triggers a popup with date presets (today, tomorrow, mon-sun) and accepts dd/mm/yyyy
Date popup filters by prefix match (d:to -> today, tomorrow)
Explicit dd/mm/yyyy input is validated and shown as selectable option
Selecting a date removes d:text from input and shows a date chip below the input
Only one date at a time — second replaces the first
On submit with popup open, Enter selects highlighted item (does not submit task)
On submit with no popup, Enter creates task with remaining text as title, labels assigned, priority and due date set
Smart parsing works in quick-add window, Backlog add-task input, and My Day add-task input
Chips below input are removable (click x or Backspace when input is empty removes last chip)
Command palette uses d: instead of due: for date filtering

stories.title: Copy task titles to clipboard
stories.description:
Add a 'Copy to clipboard' action for selected tasks, triggered by Cmd+C keyboard shortcut and available in context menus. Works in both list and kanban views, for single and multi-selected tasks.

Single task selected: Copies the task title as plain text to the clipboard (e.g., 'Buy groceries').

Multiple tasks selected: Copies all selected task titles as a bulleted markdown list, one per line, in the order they appear on screen. Format:
- Buy groceries
- Fix login bug
- Write tests

Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+C when tasks are selected and no text input is focused. Add handler in the keyboard event listeners in TaskListView and MyDayView (same keydown handler that handles ArrowUp/Down, Delete, etc.). Check that e.target is not an HTMLInputElement or HTMLTextAreaElement before intercepting. Use navigator.clipboard.writeText() for the copy operation.

Context menu integration: Add a 'Copy' menu item to the single-task ContextMenu (after Duplicate, before Delete). Add a 'Copy' menu item to the BulkContextMenu (after Snooze, before Delete). Both use the same copy logic.

Multi-task order: When copying multiple tasks, preserve the visual order (top to bottom as displayed on screen). Use the flatTasks array ordering from the view to determine order, filter to only selected task IDs.

Toast feedback: After copying, show a brief non-persistent toast. Single task: 'Copied'. Multiple tasks: 'Copied N tasks'. Use the existing useToast().addToast() with default variant.

Edge cases: No tasks selected — Cmd+C does nothing (falls through to default browser behavior). Text input focused — Cmd+C does normal text copy, not task copy. Empty selection after view switch — no-op.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Cmd+C with one task selected copies its title as plain text
Cmd+C with multiple tasks selected copies titles as a bulleted list with '- ' prefix
Multi-task copy preserves visual order (top to bottom as displayed)
Copy action appears in single-task context menu
Copy action appears in bulk context menu
Brief toast confirms the copy ('Copied' / 'Copied N tasks')
Cmd+C does nothing when no tasks are selected
Cmd+C does normal text copy when a text input is focused
Works in both list and kanban views

stories.title: Replace Backlog with per-project sidebar navigation
stories.description:
Remove the Backlog view and project switcher dropdown entirely. Replace with per-project navigation items listed directly in the sidebar. This is a major navigation restructure affecting the sidebar, view system, My Day view, AppLayout, keyboard shortcuts, and settings.

Sidebar restructure: Modify Sidebar.tsx to replace the single 'Backlog' nav item with a 'PROJECTS' section. The section has a text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.3em] text-muted header label 'PROJECTS'. Below it, render project nav items in user-defined order (stored as sidebar_order integer field on each project row in the database — add via migration). Show the top 5 projects by sidebar_order ASC. Each project nav item shows: a color dot (from project.color), the project name, and a task count badge (count of non-archived, non-template, top-level tasks in that project). Active project uses the same selection style as other nav items: bg-accent/12 border-l-2 border-accent/15. Below the top 5, show a 'More' text button (text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest text-muted hover:text-foreground cursor-pointer). Clicking 'More' expands to show all remaining projects inline, text changes to 'Less'. Clicking 'Less' collapses back to top 5. Below the projects section (after More/Less), show Archive and Templates nav items as before. Remove the old 'Backlog' nav item from VIEW_ITEMS entirely.

ViewId and view store changes: In viewStore.ts, change the ViewId type from 'my-day' | 'backlog' | 'archive' | 'templates' to 'my-day' | 'project' | 'archive' | 'templates'. Add selectedProjectId: string | null to the view store state. Add setSelectedProject(projectId: string): void action that sets currentView: 'project' and selectedProjectId: projectId. Update VIEW_ORDER array for prev/next view cycling. Update VIEW_TITLES to use the selected project name dynamically instead of a static 'Backlog' string.

AppLayout changes: In AppLayout.tsx, replace the currentView === 'backlog' conditional with currentView === 'project'. Instead of rendering TaskListView with projectId from the project store, render it using selectedProjectId from the view store. Remove the ProjectSwitcher component from the header. The view title for project view should be the selected project's name (uppercase, matching the text-3xl font-light tracking-[0.15em] uppercase typography). Remove import and usage of ProjectSwitcher.

Per-project layout memory: Each project remembers whether it was last viewed in list or kanban mode. Store in the settings table as key project_layout_{projectId} with value 'list' or 'kanban'. When switching to a project, read this setting and set layoutMode in the view store accordingly. When the user toggles Cmd+L, save the new layout for the current project. Modify toggleLayoutMode() in viewStore to also persist via setSetting('project_layout_' + selectedProjectId, newMode). On initial project selection, default to 'list' if no setting exists.

Keyboard shortcuts: Update the global keyboard handler in AppLayout: Cmd+1 = My Day (unchanged), Cmd+2 = project view (selects the topmost project by sidebar_order), Cmd+3 = Archive (was Cmd+3), Cmd+4 = Templates (was Cmd+4). When currentView === 'project', Tab cycles to the next project in sidebar order, Shift+Tab cycles to the previous. Tab/Shift+Tab wraps around at the ends. This Tab handling should be in the AppLayout keydown handler, checking that the target is not an input/textarea before intercepting.

My Day view changes — grouping by project: Modify MyDayView.tsx to group tasks by project first, then by status within each project. Instead of using a single project's statuses, load statuses for each project that has tasks in My Day. The rendering structure becomes: for each project that has My Day tasks, render a project header (text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.3em] with project color), then render StatusSection components for that project's statuses containing only that project's My Day tasks. Use useStatusesByProject(projectId) per project group. Tasks are still filtered by is_in_my_day === 1 OR due_date starts with today, then grouped by project_id.

My Day view changes — labels: The label filter bar in My Day should aggregate labels from ALL projects that have at least one task currently visible in My Day. Modify the labelsInUse computation to collect labels across all projects represented in the My Day task list, not just the current project. If two projects have labels with the same name but different colors, show both independently.

My Day view changes — add task: The add-task input in My Day needs a small inline project selector. Add a dropdown/select element to the left of the input (or integrated into the AddTaskInput component) that shows all projects with their color dots. Defaults to the default project (is_default === 1). When submitting a task, use the selected project's ID. The dropdown should be compact — a small colored dot + project name, clicking opens a list of all projects. Style: text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest for the project name, same border/surface styling as other dropdowns.

Project count badges in sidebar: Each project nav item shows a count of its tasks. Count calculation: tasks where is_archived === 0 AND is_template === 0 AND parent_id IS NULL AND project_id = this project. Same parent_id filter as the existing status section count fix. The My Day count remains cross-project as before.

Settings > Projects — drag reorder: In the projects tab in UnifiedSettingsModal, add drag handles to the project list using @dnd-kit (same SortableContext pattern as label reordering). Dragging reorders the sidebar_order field. Persist to database via a batch update. The order in settings matches the order in the sidebar.

Database migration: Add a new migration that: (1) adds sidebar_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 column to the projects table, (2) seeds the initial order based on created_at or alphabetical order.

Remove: Delete ProjectSwitcher.tsx component and all imports/usages. Remove 'backlog' from ViewId type. Remove any backlog-specific logic in AppLayout, Sidebar, and view store.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar shows My Day, then a PROJECTS section with top 5 projects, then Archive and Templates
More expands to show all projects inline, Less collapses to top 5
Clicking a project loads its tasks in the main view with the project name as title
Project nav items show color dot, name, and task count badge
Project order is configurable via drag-and-drop in Settings > Projects using @dnd-kit
Project order persists to database via sidebar_order field
Cmd+2 opens project view with topmost project selected
Tab/Shift+Tab cycles through projects when in project view
Each project remembers its list/kanban layout preference (persisted in settings table)
Cmd+L toggles and saves layout per project
My Day groups tasks by project first, then by status within each project
Each project group in My Day has a project name header with project color
My Day label filter aggregates labels from all projects with tasks in My Day
My Day add-task input has an inline project selector dropdown defaulting to default project
Backlog view and ProjectSwitcher component are fully removed
Cmd+1 = My Day, Cmd+2 = Projects, Cmd+3 = Archive, Cmd+4 = Templates
Database migration adds sidebar_order to projects table
